codeReview.summary: The feature correctly aligns exchange volume chart responses with persisted live refresh points, preserves granular recent windows, validates explicit ranges, and keeps unknown-exchange failures consistent across both volume routes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `runtime-provider-worker` skill tells workers to write failing tests first, but this worker reasonably landed the regression coverage and implementation in one change after tracing the runtime path. The skill may be too rigid for small route/service fixes where characterization already exists nearby.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `skillFeedback.deviations[0]` says the worker added regression tests and implementation in the same change set; `.factory/skills/runtime-provider-worker/SKILL.md` step 2 currently requires a failing-test-first cycle.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `695feb7`. The code now serves `/exchanges/{id}/volume_chart*` directly from chronological persisted live refresh points, and the added app regressions cover unknown-exchange 404s plus one-day/seven-day/range ownership behavior. I found no blocking correctness issues in scope.
